title: Ethernet Switches
learning_outcomes: Describe structural characteristics of Ethernet Switches.
Derive mathematical and programmatic bounds for MAC table learning loops, VLAN tagging protocols, and collision domains segregation.

content_sections.title: Ethernet Switches
content_sections.content:
### Channel Capacity & Protocol Handshakes
Computer networks coordinate transmission across physical and logical barriers, bound by mathematical limits.

#### Channel Capacity Limits
*   **Nyquist Limit (No Noise):** Max bit rate $C = 2B \log_2 L$ where $B$ is bandwidth and $L$ is signal levels.
*   **Shannon Limit (With Noise):** Max theoretical bit rate:
$$C = B \log_2(1 + \text{SNR})$$
where $\text{SNR} = S/N$ is the Signal-to-Noise Ratio (often converted from decibels: $\text{SNR}_{\text{dB}} = 10 \log_{10}(S/N)$).

#### TCP Congestion Control Loops
TCP maintains network health using a congestion window (`cwnd`):
1.  **Slow Start:** Doubles `cwnd` every round-trip time (RTT) until reaching `ssthresh` ($cwnd = cwnd \times 2$).
2.  **Congestion Avoidance:** Increments `cwnd` linearly ($cwnd = cwnd + 1$ per RTT) to probe bandwidth limits.
3.  **Fast Recovery:** Triggers on receiving 3 duplicate ACKs. Instead of dropping to 1 segment, TCP cuts `ssthresh` in half, sets `cwnd` to the new `ssthresh`, and executes linear recovery.

#### TLS 1.3 Handshake Optimization
TLS 1.3 reduces handshake latency to 1 RTT by negotiating key exchanges and cipher parameters in a single step using Diffie-Hellman Ephemeral (ECDHE) keys passed within the initial `ClientHello` payload.

### Depth Analysis: Ethernet Switches
In production configurations, we analyze how MAC table learning loops, VLAN tagging protocols, and collision domains segregation affects performance parameters. We evaluate memory layouts and consensus thresholds.

callouts.title: Common Trap in Ethernet Switches
callouts.content: A common mistake when analyzing Ethernet Switches is ignoring latency bounds and boundary edge cases during concurrent executions.

callouts.title: Production Scaling for Ethernet Switches
callouts.content: Production servers implement strict resource isolation and cache alignments to prevent performance regressions when running Ethernet Switches loops.
